001/*

003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.assertions;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.assertions.AssertionPredicate.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
023
024import java.text.*;
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.function.*;
027import java.util.regex.*;
028
029import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp.*;
031
032/**
033 * Predefined {@link AssertionPredicate} objects.
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * Typically used wherever predicates are allowed for testing of {@link Assertion} objects such as...
037 * <ul>
038 *    <li>{@link FluentObjectAssertion#is(Predicate)}
039 *    <li>{@link FluentArrayAssertion#is(Predicate...)}
040 *    <li>{@link FluentPrimitiveArrayAssertion#is(Predicate...)}
041 *    <li>{@link FluentListAssertion#isEach(Predicate...)}
042 * </ul>
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
045 * <p class='bjava'>
046 *    <jc>// Asserts that a list contains te specified values.</jc>
047 *    List&lt;Object&gt; <jv>myList</jv> = AList.<jsm>of</jsm>(...);
048 *    <jsm>assertList</jsm>(<jv>myList</jv>)
049 *       .is(<jsm>eq</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>), <jsm>any</jsm>(), <jsm>match</jsm>(<js>"bar*"</js>));
050 * </p>
051 *
052 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5>
053 * <ul>
054 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauEcosystemOverview">Juneau Ecosystem Overview</a>
055 * </ul>
056 */
057@SuppressWarnings({
058   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
059})
060public class AssertionPredicates {
061
062   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
063   private static final String ARG_expression = "expression";
064   private static final String ARG_type = "type";
065   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
066
067   private static final Function<Object,String> TYPENAME = Utils::cn;
068
069   private static final Messages MESSAGES = Messages.of(AssertionPredicates.class, "Messages");
070   private static final String MSG_valueWasNull = MESSAGES.getString("valueWasNull");
071   private static final String MSG_valueWasNotNull = MESSAGES.getString("valueWasNotNull");
072   private static final String MSG_valueDidNotMatchExpected = MESSAGES.getString("valueDidNotMatchExpected");
073   private static final String MSG_valueDidNotContainExpected = MESSAGES.getString("valueDidNotContainExpected");
074   private static final String MSG_valueUnexpectedlyMatched = MESSAGES.getString("valueUnexpectedlyMatched");
075   private static final String MSG_valueWasNotExpectedType = MESSAGES.getString("valueWasNotExpectedType");
076   private static final String MSG_valueDidNotMatchPattern = MESSAGES.getString("valueDidNotMatchPattern");
077
078   /**
079    * Combines the specified predicates into a singled AND'ed predicate.
080    *
081    * <p>
082    * Assertion error message is <js>"Predicate test #x failed."</js> followed by
083    * the inner failed message if the failed predicate extends from {@link AssertionPredicate}.
084    *
085    * @param <T> The predicate type.
086    * @param predicates The predicates to combine.
087    * @return The combined predicates.
088    */
089   @SafeVarargs
090   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> and(Predicate<T>...predicates) {
091      return new AssertionPredicate.And<>(predicates);
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * Predicate that always returns <jk>true</jk>.
096    *
097    * <p>
098    * Note that this typically has the same affect as a <jk>null</jk> predicate.
099    *
100    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
101    * @return A new predicate.
102    */
103   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> any() {
104      return test(x -> true, null);
105   }
106
107   /**
108    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value converted to a string contains the specified substring.
109    *
110    * <p>
111    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value did not contain expected.  Expected='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
112    *
113    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
114    * @param value The specified value.
115    * @return A new predicate.
116    */
117   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> contains(String value) {
118      return test(x -> StringUtils.contains(s(x), value), MSG_valueDidNotContainExpected, value, VALUE);  // NOAI
119   }
120
121   /**
122    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value equals the specified value.
123    *
124    * <p>
125    * Uses standard Java equality for testing.
126    *
127    * <p>
128    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value did not match expected.  Expected='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
129    *
130    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
131    * @param value The specified value.
132    * @return A new predicate.
133    */
134   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> eq(Object value) {
135      return test(x -> Objects.equals(x, value), MSG_valueDidNotMatchExpected, value, VALUE);
136   }
137
138   /**
139    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value converted to a string matches the specified value.
140    *
141    * <p>
142    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value did not match expected.  Expected='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
143    *
144    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
145    * @param value The specified value.
146    * @return A new predicate.
147    */
148   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> eq(String value) {
149      return test(x -> Objects.equals(s(x), value), MSG_valueDidNotMatchExpected, value, VALUE);
150   }
151
152   /**
153    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value converted to a string does not match the specified value ignoring case.
154    *
155    * <p>
156    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value did not match expected.  Expected='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
157    *
158    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
159    * @param value The specified value.
160    * @return A new predicate.
161    */
162   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> eqic(String value) {
163      return test(x -> Utils.eqic(s(x), value), MSG_valueDidNotMatchExpected, value, VALUE);  // NOAI
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value is exactly specified type.
168    *
169    * <p>
170    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value was not expected type.  Expected='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
171    *
172    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
173    * @param type The specified type.
174    * @return A new predicate.
175    */
176   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> exactType(Class<?> type) {
177      assertArgNotNull(ARG_type, type);
178      return test(x -> x != null && x.getClass().equals(type), MSG_valueWasNotExpectedType, cn(type), TYPENAME);
179   }
180
181   /**
182    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value is null.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value was not null."</js>.
186    *
187    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
188    * @return A new predicate.
189    */
190   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> isNull() { return test(x -> x == null, MSG_valueWasNotNull); }
191
192   /**
193    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value converted to a string matches the specified match pattern.
194    *
195    * <p>
196    * Match pattern can contain the <js>"*"</js> meta-character.
197    *
198    * <p>
199    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value did not match pattern.  Pattern='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
200    *
201    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
202    * @param value The specified value.
203    * @return A new predicate.
204    */
205   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> match(String value) {
206      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
207      var p = getMatchPattern(value);
208      return test(x -> x != null && p.matcher(s(x)).matches(), MSG_valueDidNotMatchPattern, value, VALUE);
209   }
210
211   /**
212    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value does not match the specified value.
213    *
214    * <p>
215    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value unexpectedly matched.  Value='{0}'."</js>.
216    *
217    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
218    * @param value The specified value.
219    * @return A new predicate.
220    */
221   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> ne(Object value) {
222      return test(x -> ! Objects.equals(x, value), MSG_valueUnexpectedlyMatched, VALUE);
223   }
224
225   /**
226    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value converted to a string does not match the specified value.
227    *
228    * <p>
229    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value unexpectedly matched.  Value='{0}'."</js>.
230    *
231    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
232    * @param value The specified value.
233    * @return A new predicate.
234    */
235   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> ne(String value) {
236      return test(x -> ! Objects.equals(s(x), value), MSG_valueUnexpectedlyMatched, VALUE);
237   }
238
239   /**
240    * Negates the specified predicate.
241    *
242    * <p>
243    * Assertion error message is <js>"Predicate test unexpectedly passed."</js>.
244    *
245    * @param <T> The predicate type.
246    * @param predicate The predicate to negate.
247    * @return The combined predicates.
248    */
249   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> not(Predicate<T> predicate) {
250      return new AssertionPredicate.Not<>(predicate);
251   }
252
253   /**
254    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value is not null.
255    *
256    * <p>
257    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value was null."</js>.
258    *
259    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
260    * @return A new predicate.
261    */
262   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> notNull() {
263      return test(x -> x != null, MSG_valueWasNull);
264   }
265
266   /**
267    * Combines the specified predicates into a singled OR'ed predicate.
268    *
269    * <p>
270    * Assertion error message is <js>"No predicate tests passed."</js>.
271    *
272    * @param <T> The predicate type.
273    * @param predicates The predicates to combine.
274    * @return The combined predicates.
275    */
276   @SafeVarargs
277   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> or(Predicate<T>...predicates) {
278      return new AssertionPredicate.Or<>(predicates);
279   }
280
281   /**
282    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value converted to a string matches the specified regular expression.
283    *
284    * <p>
285    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value did not match pattern.  Pattern='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
286    *
287    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
288    * @param value The regular expression to match.
289    * @return A new predicate.
290    */
291   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> regex(Pattern value) {
292      assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
293      return test(x -> x != null && value.matcher(s(x)).matches(), MSG_valueDidNotMatchPattern, value.pattern(), VALUE);
294   }
295
296   /**
297    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value converted to a string matches the specified regular expression.
298    *
299    * <p>
300    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value did not match pattern.  Pattern='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
301    *
302    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
303    * @param expression The regular expression to match.
304    * @return A new predicate.
305    */
306   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> regex(String expression) {
307      assertArgNotNull(ARG_expression, expression);
308      var p = Pattern.compile(expression);
309      return test(x -> x != null && p.matcher(s(x)).matches(), MSG_valueDidNotMatchPattern, expression, VALUE);
310   }
311
312   /**
313    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value converted to a string matches the specified regular expression.
314    *
315    * <p>
316    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value did not match pattern.  Pattern='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
317    *
318    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
319    * @param expression The regular expression to match.
320    * @param flags Match flags, a bit mask that may include:
321    *    <ul>
322    *       <li>{@link Pattern#CASE_INSENSITIVE CASE_INSENSITIVE}
323    *       <li>{@link Pattern#MULTILINE MULTILINE}
324    *       <li>{@link Pattern#DOTALL DOTALL}
325    *       <li>{@link Pattern#UNICODE_CASE UNICODE_CASE}
326    *       <li>{@link Pattern#CANON_EQ CANON_EQ}
327    *       <li>{@link Pattern#UNIX_LINES UNIX_LINES}
328    *       <li>{@link Pattern#LITERAL LITERAL}
329    *       <li>{@link Pattern#UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS}
330    *       <li>{@link Pattern#COMMENTS COMMENTS}
331    * @return A new predicate.
332    */
333   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> regex(String expression, int flags) {
334      assertArgNotNull(ARG_expression, expression);
335      var p = Pattern.compile(expression, flags);
336      return test(x -> x != null && p.matcher(s(x)).matches(), MSG_valueDidNotMatchPattern, expression, VALUE);
337   }
338
339   /**
340    * Predicate that wraps another predicate.
341    *
342    * <p>
343    * If the predicate extends from {@link AssertionPredicate}, the assertion error
344    * message is <js>"Value did not pass test."</js> followed by the inner assertion error.
345    * Otherwise the message is <js>"Value did not pass test.  Value='{0}'."</js>
346    *
347    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
348    * @param predicate The predicate to run.
349    * @return A new predicate.
350    */
351   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> test(Predicate<T> predicate) {
352      return new AssertionPredicate<>(predicate, null);
353   }
354
355   /**
356    * Predicate that wraps another predicate.
357    *
358    * <p>
359    * If the message specified is <jk>null</jk> and the predicate extends from {@link AssertionPredicate}, the assertion error
360    * message is <js>"Value did not pass test."</js> followed by the inner assertion error.
361    * Otherwise the message is <js>"Value did not pass test.  Value='{0}'."</js>
362    *
363    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
364    * @param predicate The predicate to run.
365    * @param msg
366    *    The error message if predicate fails.
367    *    <br>Supports {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
368    * @param args
369    *    Optional message arguments.
370    *    <br>Can contain {@code #VALUE} to specify the value itself as an argument.
371    *    <br>Can contain {@link Function functions} to apply to the tested value.
372    * @return A new predicate.
373    */
374   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> test(Predicate<T> predicate, String msg, Object...args) {
375      return new AssertionPredicate<>(predicate, msg, args);
376   }
377
378   /**
379    * Predicate that returns <jk>true</jk> if the tested value is the specified or child type.
380    *
381    * <p>
382    * Assertion error message is <js>"Value was not expected type.  Expected='{0}', Actual='{1}'."</js>.
383    *
384    * @param <T> The object type being tested.
385    * @param type The specified type.
386    * @return A new predicate.
387    */
388   public static final <T> AssertionPredicate<T> type(Class<?> type) {
389      assertArgNotNull(ARG_type, type);
390      return test(x -> x != null && type.isAssignableFrom(x.getClass()), MSG_valueWasNotExpectedType, cn(type), TYPENAME);
391   }
392
393   /**
394    * Constructor.
395    */
396   protected AssertionPredicates() {} // HTT - utility class; instantiation via subclass is theoretical
397}
